answer:
Pumble API authentication typically requires two headers for every request: x-app-token and token [1][2]. The x-app-token is your application's unique identifier [1][3], while the token is the access token generated for a specific user or bot [1][2].

For applications, Pumble uses OAuth2 [4][5]. To obtain an access token, you redirect the user to the Pumble consent screen [4][5]. After the user authorizes, they are redirected to your specified URL with a code parameter [4][5]. You then exchange this code, along with your clientSecret, at the endpoint below to receive the access token [4][5]:

```
POST https://api-ga.pumble.com/oauth2/access
```

Alternatively, the Pumble API addon allows users to generate API keys directly within the Pumble workspace for simpler, direct requests [6].
